chapter_title.en: Chapter 56 – The silence and preservation of the tongue

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (211/ 8)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (211/ 8)
text: شَهِدْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) وَ هُوَ يَقُولُ لِمَوْلًى لَهُ يُقَالُ لَهُ سَالِمٌ وَ وَضَعَ يَدَهُ عَلَى شَفَتَيْهِ وَ قَالَ يَا سَالِمُ احْفَظْ لِسَانَكَ تَسْلَمْ وَ لَا تَحْمِلِ النَّاسَ عَلَى رِقَابِنَا .

en.hubeali: From him, from Al Hassan Bin Mahboub, from Abu Ali Al Jawwany who said,

‘I witnessed Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, and he<sup>asws</sup> was saying to a slave of his<sup>asws</sup> called Saalim, and he<sup>asws</sup> placed his<sup>asws</sup> hand upon his lips and said: ‘O Saalim! Preserve your tongue, you will be safe, and do not load the people upon our<sup>asws</sup> necks’.
en.sarwar: 3. It is narrated from him (narrator of the Hadith above) from al-Hassan ibn Mahbub from abu Ali al-Jawwani who has said the following: “I saw abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) speaking to one of his slaves called Salim, placing his hands over his (slaves) mouth, ‘O Salim, control your tongue and you will have peace and do not load people over our necks.’”
